stories.story: FBI agents and task force officers were following 36-year-old Kevone Charleston of Austell, Georgia, an armed robbery suspect, early Saturday. Kevone Charleston pulled into a CVS pharmacy in Forsyth County, Georgia, around 7 o'clock Saturday morning. Authorities say 36-year-old Kevone Charleston parked his vehicle nearby and popped the hood, then walked to the CVS, preparing to enter. When agents confronted 36-year-old Kevone Charleston, he ran, got in his car, and traveled about 75 yards as agents opened fire. Twelve FBI agents and six government vehicles followed 36-year-old Kevone Charleston.
key_facts: Kevone Charleston, 36, of Austell, Georgia, is an armed robbery suspect.
FBI agents and task force officers followed Kevone Charleston to a CVS pharmacy in Forsyth County, Georgia.
The incident occurred around 7 o'clock Saturday morning.
Kevone Charleston fled in his car when confronted by agents.
Twelve FBI agents and six government vehicles were involved in the pursuit.

stories.story: During his attempt to flee, 36-year-old Kevone Charleston of Austell, Georgia, an armed robbery suspect, rammed several FBI vehicles. FBI Special Agent Stephen Emmett said 36-year-old Kevone Charleston collided with the first government vehicle, the Taurus, then the second, causing an SUV to end on its side. One government vehicle sustained heavy damage to its front and side, and another government SUV ended up on its side. That's how two FBI agents sustained their injuries. The two injured FBI agents were treated at an area hospital and released, and FBI Special Agent Stephen Emmett says "they are fine."
key_facts: Kevone Charleston, 36, of Austell, Georgia, rammed several FBI vehicles.
Two FBI agents were injured in a crash involving Kevone Charleston's vehicle.
One government vehicle sustained heavy damage, and another government SUV ended on its side.
The injured FBI agents were treated at an area hospital and released.
FBI Special Agent Stephen Emmett confirmed the agents are fine.
explicit_unknowns: The names of the injured agents.
The exact location of the area hospital.

stories.story: After ramming FBI vehicles and injuring two FBI agents, 36-year-old Kevone Charleston of Austell, Georgia, an armed robbery suspect, was stopped 8 feet away in the median. Kevone Charleston received gunshot wounds at that location. Kevone Charleston was shot and wounded by FBI agents and task force officers, but his injuries are not life-threatening, according to Forsyth County Sheriff's Deputy Robin Regan. FBI Special Agent Stephen Emmett said 36-year-old Kevone Charleston "was trying to get away, our agents were trying to stop him."
key_facts: Kevone Charleston, 36, of Austell, Georgia, was stopped 8 feet away in the median.
Kevone Charleston received gunshot wounds and was shot by FBI agents and task force officers.
Kevone Charleston's injuries are not life-threatening.
Forsyth County Sheriff's Deputy Robin Regan provided information on Kevone Charleston's injuries.

stories.story: 36-year-old Kevone Charleston of Austell, Georgia, an armed robbery suspect, is suspected of involvement in 32 commercial robberies dating to November 2013. FBI Special Agent Stephen Emmett said it was an intensive investigation that was already underway as a priority for the FBI's violent crimes and major offender squad. FBI Special Agent Stephen Emmett added that 36-year-old Kevone Charleston's MO involved armed confrontations, so officers went into this fully prepared for an armed confrontation based on his past history. The incident was the "conclusion of an intensive and lengthy investigation." FBI Special Agent Stephen Emmett is relieved that the FBI's officers and agents are OK and that 36-year-old Kevone Charleston is in custody.
key_facts: Kevone Charleston, 36, of Austell, Georgia, is suspected of 32 commercial robberies dating to November 2013.
The investigation was intensive and a priority for the FBI's violent crimes and major offender squad.
Kevone Charleston's MO involved armed confrontations.
The incident concluded an intensive and lengthy investigation.
CNN's Vivian Kuo and Ryan Scallan contributed to this report.
